This release drops support for Python 3.9, which reached [end of life in October 2025](https://devguide.python.org/versions/). The minimum supported Python version is now 3.10. There are no runtime API or behavior changes — if you're on Python 3.10 or later, `pip install --upgrade tracking-markers` works without any changes to your code.

### Changes

- **Drop Python 3.9 support.** `requires-python` is now `>=3.10,<3.13`.

### Maintenance

- Bumped the dev-only `pytest` dependency to `>=9.0.3,<10` to address a security advisory in pytest 7.x/8.x. End users are unaffected — `pytest` is a dev dependency and is not installed when you `pip install tracking-markers`.

**Full changelog:** https://github.com/bertoldi-collab/tracking-markers/compare/v0.11.0...v0.12.0

Enjoy! :octocat:
